intended_use_full: The SensiCardiac is an electronic auscultatory device, intended to provide support to the physician in the evaluation of patients' heart sounds. The product acquires and records the acoustic signals of the heart and analyzes these signals. The analysis procedure will identify specific heart sounds that may be present. Identified sounds include S1, S2, and suspected murmurs. The device is indicated for use in a clinical setting, by a physician or by trained personnel who are acting on the orders of a licensed physician. It is not intended as a sole means of diagnosis.
target_condition: Heart murmurs and abnormal heart sounds
ground_truth: Recorded pathological conditions and clinical diagnosis (cleft lips and palates screening study)
other_metrics: Zargis Cardioscan achieved 67.9% accuracy; Sensi V1 achieved unspecified accuracy in comparative study; usability validation scores averaged 4 out of 5; 56 heart sounds accepted criteria of good quality in pediatric validation
